{{Quote|The Death Watch hideout can be found on Endor in the northwestern part of the planet. Be cautious and bring your friends with you. I hear they do not take prisoners and enjoying killing intruders.|[[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[D'krn]], to a spacer|<ref name="SWG">{{GalaxiesAED}}</ref>}}
The '''Death Watch bunker''' was the primary location of the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Death Watch/Legends|Death Watch]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].

==History==
The Death Watch established a bunker on the [[Moon/Legends|forest moon]] of [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] where it would be virtually invisible to the rest of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Death Watch [[Wraith (Death Watch)|Wraiths]], [[Ghost (Death Watch)|Ghosts]], and [[Bloodguard]]s continued operating in secrecy, contracting with [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] as [[Assassin/Legends|assassins]] in exchange for the crime syndicate helping them further their goals.

By [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]], the Death Watch had fallen into obscurity, known mostly as an ancient Mandalorian splinter group whose armor had inspired the uniforms of the [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Emperor's Royal Guard]]. However, while working for [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], famed [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] discovered that the group was not merely the stuff of myth and rumor but was in fact operating out of a hidden location. Still under contract to remain with Jabba, Fett hired a group of [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]] to investigate for him.

At the same time, the Death Watch's [[Overlord|leader]], an [[unidentified Death Watch Overlord]], came to the attention of [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[D'krn]], stationed in Tatooine's [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Bestine (city)/Legends|Bestine]]. Wishing to use the Overlord as the [[Clone template/Legends|prime clone]] for a new and improved batch of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]], D'krn also hired a group of spacers loyal to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] to discover the Mandalorian's whereabouts. These spacers killed the Overlord and his guards and took a sample of his blood to return to D'krn.

A short time later, the Hutt [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] Jabba Desilijic Tiure learned of the existence of the Death Watch bunker. Furthermore, the Hutt discovered that the Black Sun was intending to expand its influence. Jabba therefore paid an informant in Black Sun named [[Stanic Wavingstar]] to spy on its activities. In addition, one of Jabba's minions, the [[Trandoshan/Legends|Trandoshan]] bounty hunter [[Bossk/Legends|Bossk]], recruited a spacer to attack Black Sun enforcers at the Death Watch bunker in order to test the strength of the Black Sun.<ref name="SWG:TI">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=general|Troublesome Influences|Tatooine}}</ref>

==Contingent==
Defenses were top priority within the bunker. To eliminate anything that could reveal their secrecy, the Death Watch formed a coalition with Black Sun for added protection. The organic guards were supplemented by modified [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1]], [[B2-series super battle droid/Legends|B2]], and [[Blastromech droid|Blastromech]] [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] patrolling the corridors. Deep below the bunker, the [[Mining/Legends|mines]] were run by [[Foreman/Legends|Foreman]] [[Japer Witter]], who oversaw several [[Miner/Legends|miners]], [[Scientist/Legends|scientists]], and [[Technician/Legends|technicians]]. Also roaming the mines were several wild [[mine rat]]s that would attack intruders on sight.

==Layout==
The bunker was divided into three main areas: Section A, Section B, and the [[alum]] mines. Access to each of these areas was strictly controlled, and the only passkeys were carried by the Overlord's three lieutenants: [[Rageon Vart]], [[Klin Nif]], and [[Fenri Dalso]] respectively. Security terminals in each section were equipped with alarm devices that would activate, alerting the Death Watch and Black Sun guards, should any unauthorized access be attempted. Located in the mines were several workshops, making the bunker one of the only places in the galaxy that had proper equipment to produce the famed [[Mandalorian armor/Legends|Mandalorian armor]]. In exchange for their protective services, the Black Sun organization used the Mandalorians' superior armor-smithing techniques to develop the [[Crusader Mark III armor]] inside the installation.

==Behind the scenes==
The Death Watch bunker was a location in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'', a [[Wikipedia:Massively multiplayer online role-playing game|massively multiplayer online role-playing game]] developed by [[Sony]] Online Entertainment and published by [[LucasArts]],<ref name="SWG:DWB" /> prior to its closure on [[December 15]], [[2011]].<ref name="Shutdown">{{SonyCite|url=playerinfo|text=IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT ''STAR WARS'' GALAXIES™}}</ref> The Death Watch bunker was added to the game with an [[W:c:swg:Update Notes: 25 May 04|update]] released on [[May 25]], [[2004]].<ref name="SonyCite-Publish 8: Droids Rebuilt">{{SonyCite|url=recent_updates.jsp?o=61943|text=Publish 8: Droids Rebuilt}}</ref> In the game, players must descend through the dangerous depths of the bunker to craft their own suit of Mandalorian armor.
